source_jobs.title: Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About depthfirst

We believe that software is the foundation of modern civilization - yet vulnerabilities threaten its integrity, security, and resilience. We are on a mission to solve security.

depthfirst is building intelligence to detect and remediate critical software vulnerabilities. We are training and scaling security AI agents to discover zero-days vulnerabilities across large customer codebases and popular open source software.

Our founding team includes deep expertise in data, infrastructure, security and LLMs with technical leaders from DeepMind, Databricks, Square, and Faire. We are looking for strong technical people who are interested in working at the intersection of AI, Security, and Infrastructure.



About this role

We’re seeking an experienced front-end focused Product Engineer to lead the charge in building the world’s first AI-native platform that autonomously finds and fixes software vulnerabilities in your code. You will work on developing and designing our web app, already serving many large customers, finding and remediating vulnerabilities at scale. You’ll play a central role in driving the product, understanding the user, and reimagining how companies do security.



You’re excited about this role because you will…

 - Develop and Design delightful AI-first products that solve real challenges. Helping organizations of any size avoid the next big leak.

 - Drive Web Performance Optimization: You'll take ownership of front-end performance, ensuring depthfirst's website meets and exceeds industry benchmarks for speed and responsiveness.

 - Leverage Cutting-Edge Technologies: Work extensively with Next.js, React, and modern web development practices to create robust, scalable, and performant solutions.

 - Work on a Product that Solves a Critical Problem - and we already have large customers using us who have found some eye-opening vulnerabilities within the first few days of using our product.
   
   

Qualifications

 - 3+ years of professional experience in front-end engineering, with a deep understanding of React and JavaScript/TypeScript, Postgres (Neon).

 - Knowledge of Next.js is a bonus.

 - Proven experience optimizing large-scale web applications for performance, scalability, and maintainability.

 - Expertise in developing reusable UI frameworks, libraries, and tools.

 - Familiarity with state management libraries (e.g., Redux, Zustand) and modern CSS frameworks (e.g., Tailwind CSS).

 - Strong understanding of web fundamentals: HTTP, browser rendering pipelines, and web performance metrics.

 - Attention to detail, product mindset and a desire to build user-centric products
   
   

What We Offer

 - Competitive Salary with meaningful equity

 - Health, Vision, and Dental Insurance

 - Office lunch and dinner (when working out of San Francisco office)



depthfirst is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition, or any other basis protected by law.



To all recruitment agencies: depthfirst does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to depthfirst employees. depthfirst is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes and will not pay fees to any third-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement with the Company.

source_jobs.title: Security Researcher
source_jobs.description:
About depthfirst

We believe that software is the foundation of modern civilization - yet vulnerabilities threaten its integrity, security, and resilience. We are on a mission to solve security.

depthfirst is building intelligence to detect and remediate critical software vulnerabilities. We are training and scaling security AI agents to discover zero-days vulnerabilities across large customer codebases and popular open source software.

Our founding team includes deep expertise in data, infrastructure, security and LLMs with technical leaders from DeepMind, Databricks, Square, and Faire. We are looking for strong technical people who are interested in working at the intersection of AI, Security, and Infrastructure.



About this role

We’re seeking an experienced Security Researcher to join our effort in building and training AI agents for vulnerability discovery and exploitation.

We are building a technology capable of finding the next Log4J at scale, finding and remediating vulnerabilities in customer and open source codebases.

We are looking for strong security researchers with strong intuition to identify, analyze, and investigate application vulnerabilities. You’ll collaborate with AI researchers and engineers to uncover novel attack vectors and contribute to the development of advanced detection and defense capabilities. Your work will play a crucial role in building a product that aims to redefine how companies do security.



You’re excited about this role because you will…

 - Build a technology capable of finding novel vulnerabilities at scale both in open source and proprietary codebases

 - Develop techniques to reduce false positives leveraging automated exploitation, proof of concept generation and context inference

 - Work closely with engineers to understand limitations and design new methodologies to improve our system

 - Publish internal technical reports and contribute to security advisories as needed.

 - Work on a Product that Solves a Critical Problem - and we already have a handful of customers who have found it valuable in fixing some eye-opening vulnerabilities within the first few days of using our product.
   
   

Qualifications

 - 3+ years of full-time experience in security research, offensive security, or related fields.

 - Experience with finding vulnerabilities in source code

 - Experience creating PoC exploits for vulnerabilities

 - Programming experience in Python

 - A bachelor's degree in Computer Science/Software Engineering or equivalent industry experience

 - A love for technology, and an insatiable curiosity for new tools to tackle real problems

 - Capable of solving complex problems with simple solutions. Building reliable and scalable products, making right trade-offs along the way

 - A tendency to leave things in a better way than you found it
   
   

What We Offer

 - Competitive Salary with meaningful equity

 - Health, Vision, and Dental Insurance

 - Office lunch (when working from our San Francisco office)



depthfirst is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition, or any other basis protected by law.



To all recruitment agencies: depthfirst does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to depthfirst employees. depthfirst is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes and will not pay fees to any third-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement with the Company.

source_jobs.title: Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About depthfirst

We believe that software is the foundation of modern civilization - yet vulnerabilities threaten its integrity, security, and resilience. We are on a mission to solve security.

depthfirst is building intelligence to detect and remediate critical software vulnerabilities. We are training and scaling security AI agents to discover zero-days vulnerabilities across large customer codebases and popular open source software.

Our founding team includes deep expertise in data, infrastructure, security and LLMs with technical leaders from DeepMind, Databricks, Square, and Faire. We are looking for strong technical people who are interested in working at the intersection of AI, Security, and Infrastructure.



About this role:

We’re seeking an experienced Backend Engineer to lead the charge in building the world’s first AI-native platform that autonomously finds and fixes software vulnerabilities in code. You will work on infrastructure that powers our security AI agents. You’ll play a central role in designing a scalable infrastructure, solving challenges from code execution at scale, complex agent orchestration, evaluation benchmark and training pipelines. We care about engineering excellence and secure & well-architected designs that scale to thousands of enterprise-level users. Your work will play a crucial role in building a product that aims to redefine how companies do security.



You’re excited about this role because you will…

 - Be a Technical Leader: Provide mentorship, set technical direction, and define best practices for backend engineering at depthfirst.

 - Architect and Scale complex coding agent pipelines that scan and execute very large codebases to find the most intricate vulnerability.

 - Build, Maintain and Evolve evaluation benchmarks for AI agents that reflects the problems our users face

 - Take Ownership of our product, contributing to its architecture, performance, and long-term success

 - Work on a Product that Solves a Critical Problem - and we already have a handful of customers who have found it valuable in fixing some eye-opening vulnerabilities within the first few days of using our product.
   
   

Qualifications

 - 3+ years of full-time experience in backend software engineering

 - A passion for programming and solving problems with code. Building reliable and stable products to solve real user problems

 - Familiarity with multiple languages is a plus. Our stack is Typescript/Python

 - A bachelor's degree in Computer Science/Software Engineering or equivalent industry experience

 - A love for technology, and an insatiable curiosity for new tools to tackle real problems

 - Capable of solving complex problems with simple solutions. Building reliable and scalable products, making right trade-offs along the way

 - A tendency to leave things in a better way than you found it
   
   

Technologies We Use:

 - Typescript, Python

 - Temporal, React, NextJS

 - Postgres, Temporal, Redis

 - LLMs everywhere

 - AWS
   
   

What We Offer

 - Competitive Salary with meaningful equity

 - Health, Vision, and Dental Insurance

 - Office lunch and dinner (when working from our San Francisco office)



depthfirst is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition, or any other basis protected by law.



To all recruitment agencies: depthfirst does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to depthfirst employees. depthfirst is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes and will not pay fees to any third-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement with the Company.

source_jobs.title: Solutions Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About depthfirst
We believe that software is the foundation of modern civilization. Yet vulnerabilities threaten its integrity, security, and resilience. We are on a mission to secure the world’s software.

depthfirst is building intelligence to detect and remediate critical software vulnerabilities. We are training and scaling security AI agents to discover zero-day vulnerabilities across large customer codebases and popular open source software.

Our founding team brings deep expertise in data, infrastructure, security and AI, with leaders from DeepMind, Databricks, Square, and Faire. We're looking for thoughtful, driven people excited to work at the intersection of AI, Security, and Infrastructure.



About This Role

We’re looking for an Enterprise Solutions Engineer to help enterprises transform how they secure software. This is a highly strategic, customer-facing role where you will own the technical sales motion from discovery through validation and expansion.

You’ll partner directly with Account Executives, security leaders, and engineering teams to define evaluation criteria, lead technical validation, and translate results into measurable business impact. As an early member of the solutions engineering function, you will help define how we run evaluations, position differentiated value, and scale our enterprise motion.



You’re excited about this role because you will...

 - Own and lead full-cycle technical evaluations, from discovery through proof of value and executive readout

 - Partner with security and engineering stakeholders to define clear success criteria tied to vulnerability detection quality, false positive reduction, and remediation speed

 - Demonstrate how depthfirst differs from legacy SAST and SCA tools through contextual code understanding, reachability and exploitability analysis, and highly repeatable results

 - Work hands-on in customer CI/CD environments (GitHub, GitLab, Jenkins, etc.) to ensure seamless deployment and minimal developer disruption

 - Translate technical validation into executive-ready ROI narratives for CISOs, CTOs, and VPs of Engineering

 - Act as the voice of the customer to Product and Engineering, shaping roadmap priorities based on real-world feedback

 - Help build and scale a world-class solutions engineering function, including playbooks, demo environments, and repeatable evaluation frameworks
   

Qualifications

 - 4+ years of experience in a customer-facing technical role such as Solutions Engineer, Sales Engineer, Security Engineer, or similar

 - Strong understanding of modern software development workflows and CI/CD systems

 - Exposure to application security, vulnerability management, static analysis, or secure SDLC practices

 - Ability to communicate complex technical concepts clearly to both engineers and executive stakeholders

 - High ownership mentality and comfort operating in fast-moving, ambiguous startup environments

 - Mission-driven and genuinely excited about AI and security

 - Located in or willing to relocate to San Francisco (preferred)
   

What We Offer

 - Competitive salary with meaningful equity

 - Health, vision, and dental insurance

 - High ownership and impact as an early member of the go-to-market team

 - The opportunity to join a rapidly scaling AI security startup during a critical growth phase



depthfirst is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition, or any other basis protected by law.



To all recruitment agencies: depthfirst does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to depthfirst employees. depthfirst is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes and will not pay fees to any third-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement with the Company.

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
About depthfirst

We believe that software is the foundation of modern civilization. Yet vulnerabilities threaten its integrity, security, and resilience. We are on a mission to secure the world’s software.

depthfirst is building intelligence to detect and remediate critical software vulnerabilities. We are training and scaling security AI agents to discover zero-day vulnerabilities across large customer codebases and popular open source software.

Our founding team brings deep expertise in data, infrastructure, security and AI, with leaders from DeepMind, Databricks, Square, and Faire. We're looking for thoughtful, driven people excited to work at the intersection of AI, Security, and Infrastructure.



About This Role

We're looking for a hungry, high-EQ Outbound SDR to join our GTM team at an exciting stage of growth. You'll be one of the early sales hires, which means what you build here will shape how the company sells for years to come. This isn't a seat-filler role. It's a career-defining opportunity for the right person.

You'll be responsible for prospecting, engaging, and qualifying potential customers through outbound channels. You know how to have a real conversation, how to listen, and how to earn a meeting.



You're excited about this role because you will…

 - Build and execute outbound sequences across email, phone, and LinkedIn targeting ideal customer profiles

 - Research accounts and personalize outreach to drive meaningful engagement

 - Qualify prospects and book discovery calls for Account Executives

 - Collaborate closely with marketing and sales leadership to refine messaging and targeting

 - Track activity in CRM and provide feedback on what's working in the field

 - Hit and exceed monthly pipeline generation targets
   
   

Qualifications

 - Background in cybersecurity (professional, academic, or personal interest) OR at least 1 year of outbound sales experience in any industry

 - High emotional intelligence and genuine curiosity about people and their problems

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills

 - Comfort operating in a startup environment where things change, priorities shift, and resourcefulness is rewarded

 - A clear understanding of the tradeoff: early-stage comp versus long-term equity upside, and excitement about that bet

 - Coachable, self-motivated, and competitive in a collaborative way

 - Located in or willing to relocate to San Francisco (this role is in-office)
   
   

What We Offer

 - Competitive salary with meaningful equity

 - Health, vision, and dental insurance

 - Office lunch and dinner (SF office)

 - High ownership and impact: you'll define how we hire and who we become as a company

 - The opportunity to join a rapidly scaling AI startup during its most formative phase



depthfirst is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition, or any other basis protected by law.



To all recruitment agencies: depthfirst does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to depthfirst employees. depthfirst is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes and will not pay fees to any third-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement with the Company.

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
About depthfirst

We believe that software is the foundation of modern civilization - yet vulnerabilities threaten its integrity, security, and resilience. We are on a mission to solve security.

depthfirst is building intelligence to detect and remediate critical software vulnerabilities. We are training and scaling security AI agents to discover zero-days vulnerabilities across large customer codebases and popular open source software.

Our founding team includes deep expertise in data, infrastructure, security and LLMs with technical leaders from DeepMind, Databricks, Square, and Faire. We are looking for strong technically-minded people who are interested in working at the intersection of AI, Security and Infrastructure.



About this role

We’re seeking an experienced Account Executive to lead the charge in building depthfirst’s sales and account management team. This newly created role puts you at the helm of depthfirst’s most strategic enterprise accounts—owning customer success, account expansion, and meaningful business outcomes.

You’ll partner directly with senior leaders in Security and Engineering and influence how leading global enterprises build their security systems in the age of AI, with your impact measured by strong Net Revenue Retention through renewals, retention, and targeted growth.



You’re excited about this role because you will…

 - Manage and grow a portfolio of depthfirst’s most strategic enterprise accounts across the globe.

 - Develop and execute account strategies that drive long-term customer loyalty and unlock new business value.

 - Build strong, multi-level relationships with C-suite executives and senior stakeholders.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with Customer Success, Solutions Engineering, and Channel teams to identify and realize expansion opportunities.

 - Help customers integrate security seamlessly into fast-moving, AI-driven development workflows.

 - Provide strategic customer insights to Product Management to influence product direction and priorities.

 - Drive measurable results through targeted, outcome-focused account growth plans.
   
   

Qualifications

 - 5+ years of enterprise account management experience in technical SaaS - owning large, complex accounts.

 - Proven success in driving outstanding Net Revenue Retention through renewals, retention, and strategic account growth.

 - Skilled at engaging and influencing C-level executives within complex, matrixed organizations.

 - Deep expertise in Cybersecurity, DevOps, DevSecOps, or enterprise software account leadership.

 - Comfortable operating in high-growth, fast-paced environments, with a track record of building new strategies, functions, or approaches from the ground up.

 - Strong collaborator with the ability to align cross-functional teams around customer outcomes.
   
   

What We Offer

 - Competitive Salary with meaningful equity

 - Health, Vision, and Dental Insurance

 - Office lunch and dinner (when working out of San Francisco office)



depthfirst is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition, or any other basis protected by law.

source_jobs.title: Forward-Deployed Security Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About depthfirst

We believe that software is the foundation of modern civilization. Yet vulnerabilities threaten its integrity, security, and resilience. We are on a mission to secure the world's software.

depthfirst is building intelligence to detect and remediate critical software vulnerabilities. We are training and scaling security AI agents to discover zero-days vulnerabilities across large customer codebases and popular open source software.

Our founding team includes deep expertise in data, infrastructure, security and LLMs with technical leaders from DeepMind, Databricks, Square, and Faire. We are looking for strong technical people who are interested in working at the intersection of AI, Security, and Infrastructure.



About This Role

We're looking for a Forward Deployed Security Engineer to work directly with customers to deploy and operationalize depthfirst's security AI agents in their environments. You'll be the technical bridge between our product and enterprise security infrastructure, ensuring our AI successfully detects vulnerabilities at scale.

You'll embed with customers to understand their security challenges, build custom integrations on top of depthfirst's platform, and partner closely with our product team to generalize solutions. This role combines hands-on engineering with deep customer collaboration—you'll shape both how customers use depthfirst and how our product evolves.



You're excited about this role because you will…

 - Embed with enterprise customers to deploy depthfirst's security AI agents into production environments

 - Build deep relationships with security teams, becoming a trusted advisor who understands their challenges

 - Translate customer problems into solutions: build custom integrations, data pipelines, and workflows that connect depthfirst with existing security tools

 - Bridge the gap between customers and product—identify patterns across deployments and collaborate with product to turn one-off solutions into platform capabilities

 - Communicate clearly with diverse stakeholders, from security engineers to CISOs

 - Own end-to-end technical delivery: from scoping to implementation and optimization

 - Travel to customer sites as needed to drive successful outcomes



Qualifications

 - 3+ years of software engineering experience or relevant expertise

 - Excellent communication skills and genuine interest in working closely with customers to solve their problems

 - Experience in application security, penetration testing, or security research

 - Experience bridging customer needs and product capabilities, with a track record of collaborating with product teams to generalize solutions

 - Experience with security tooling, vulnerability management, or security-focused engineering

 - Proficiency in Python and experience building integrations, APIs, and data pipelines

 - Strong problem-solving skills with a bias toward action

 - Excitement about AI and security

 - Based in or willing to relocate to San Francisco



Bonus Points For

 - Experience with LLMs, AI agents, or AI platforms

 - Familiarity with enterprise security infrastructure (SIEM, SOAR, vulnerability management platforms)

 - Prior experience as a solutions engineer or forward deployed engineer

 - Open source contributions or experience with large codebases



What We Offer

 - Competitive salary with meaningful equity

 - Health, vision, and dental insurance

 - Office lunch and dinner (when working from our SF office)

 - Direct impact on customer success and product direction

 - The opportunity to work at the cutting edge of AI and security



depthfirst is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition, or any other basis protected by law.



To all recruitment agencies: depthfirst does not acce

source_jobs.title: Research Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About depthfirst

We believe that software is the foundation of modern civilization - yet vulnerabilities threaten its integrity, security, and resilience. We are on a mission to solve security.

depthfirst is building intelligence to detect and remediate critical software vulnerabilities. We are training and scaling security AI agents to discover zero-days vulnerabilities across large customer codebases and popular open source software.

Our founding team includes deep expertise in data, infrastructure, security and LLMs with technical leaders from DeepMind, Databricks, Square, and Faire. We are looking for strong technical people who are interested in working at the intersection of AI, Security, and Infrastructure.



About this role

We’re seeking an experienced Research Engineer to join our effort in building and training AI agents for vulnerability discovery and exploitation.

We are building a technology capable of finding the next Log4J at scale, finding and remediating vulnerabilities in customer and open source codebases.

We are looking for strong engineers with strong intuition, experience in model evaluation, and benchmarks. Reinforcement Learning experience is a plus. Your work will play a crucial role in building a product that aims to redefine how companies do security.



You’re excited about this role because you will…

 - Build State-of-the-Art AI Agentic pipelines optimized for discovering intricate software vulnerabilities and generate exploits

 - Design, Maintain and Evolve evaluation benchmarks for AI agents that reflects the problems our users face

 - Design & Develop training procedures and reinforcement learning environments to train security coding agents

 - Work on a Product that Solves a Critical Problem - and we already have a handful of customers who have found it valuable in fixing some eye-opening vulnerabilities within the first few days of using our product.
   
   

Qualifications

 - 3+ years of full-time experience in AI research engineering in machine learning, deep learning, and/or natural language processing

 - Experience with developing machine learning models at scale from inception to business impact

 - Programming experience in Python and hands-on experience with frameworks such as PyTorch

 - A bachelor's degree in Computer Science/Software Engineering or equivalent industry experience

 - A love for technology, and an insatiable curiosity for new tools to tackle real problems

 - Capable of solving complex problems with simple solutions. Building reliable and scalable products, making right trade-offs along the way

source_jobs.title: Business Operations Lead
source_jobs.description:
About depthfirst

We believe that software is the foundation of modern civilization. Yet vulnerabilities threaten its integrity, security, and resilience. We are on a mission to secure the world's software.

depthfirst is building intelligence to detect and remediate critical software vulnerabilities. We are training and scaling security AI agents to discover zero-day vulnerabilities across large customer codebases and popular open source software.

Our founding team brings deep expertise in data, infrastructure, security and AI, with leaders from DeepMind, Databricks, Square, and Faire. We're looking for thoughtful, driven people excited to work at the intersection of AI, Security, and Infrastructure.

About This Role

We're looking for a BizOps Lead to help us scale intelligently during one of the most exciting phases of depthfirst's growth. This is a role for someone who is hungry to learn, moves fast, and gets energy from working across a lot of different things at once, from data and analysis to operational execution to cross-functional projects.

You'll work across a wide range of workstreams within Finance and Operations, owning execution on initiatives that matter and help a fast-moving company run efficiently. No job is too big or too small. This role has significant room to grow as we scale.

You're excited about this role because you will...

 - Support analysis of key business drivers, pulling data, building models, and helping surface what's working and what isn't

 - Own execution on cross-functional projects spanning GTM, product, engineering, and beyond, keeping things moving and making sure nothing falls through the cracks

 - Drive operational initiatives that need a structured, accountable owner: vendor management, compliance workstreams, process documentation, and more

 - Be the connective tissue across teams, following up, coordinating, and getting things unstuck

 - Roll up your sleeves across whatever the business needs, equally comfortable digging into a spreadsheet and knocking out an operational task that just needs to get done

Qualifications

 - 4+ years of experience in bizops, strategy and operations, consulting, banking, or a similar role at a fast-growing SaaS or AI company

 - Strong analytical skills, comfortable with data, spreadsheets, and turning ambiguous questions into clear answers

 - Familiarity with GTM funnel metrics and a solid understanding of how B2B businesses operate and scale

 - Highly organized and reliable, you follow through, sweat the details, and don't let things drop

 - Comfortable with ambiguity and energized by building from scratch

 - Clear, direct communicator who can write well and work across different teams and functions

 - Mission-driven and genuinely excited about AI and security

 - Located in or willing to relocate to San Francisco (this role is in-office)

Bonus Points For

 - Experience at a high-growth startup or early-stage company

 - Exposure to financial modeling or pricing strategy

 - Familiarity with security, infrastructure, or developer tooling markets

What We Offer

 - Competitive salary with meaningful equity

 - Health, vision, and dental insurance

 - Office lunch and dinner (SF office)

 - Hands-on experience across finance, ops, and strategy at a rapidly scaling AI startup

 - Direct mentorship and significant room to grow as the company scales



depthfirst is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition, or any other basis protected by law.

notes: Highly technical roles, AI/ML focus, and security expertise suggest a strong engineering core.
